SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.spi

Interface TransactionTemplate

public interface TransactionTemplate
The transaction template is a functional interface that allows callers to let logic be executed in the scope of a new transaction.
Since:
1.5

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ThreadLocal<TransactionContext>
    contextHolder()
    Returns the thread local that holds the current transaction context.
    default Optional<TransactionContext>
    currentContext()
    Returns the current transaction context if any.
    <R> R
    execute(TransactionCallback<R> action, TransactionContext context)
    Executes the specified action in the scope of a transaction.
    TransactionTemplate
    isolation(int isolation)
    Set the isolation level, such as DEFAULT, REPEATABLE_READ, READ_COMMITTED, READ_UNCOMMITTED and SERIALIZABLE.
    TransactionContext
    newContext(boolean suspendMode)
    Creates a new transaction context based on the current configuration of this transaction template.
    TransactionTemplate
    propagation(String propagation)
    Set the propagation, such as DEFAULT, REQUIRED, REQUIRES_NEW, NESTED.
    TransactionTemplate
    readOnly(boolean readOnly)
    Set whether to optimize as read-only transaction.
    TransactionTemplate
    timeout(int timeoutSeconds)
    Set the transaction timeout to apply, as number of seconds.

  • Method Details

    • propagation

      TransactionTemplate propagation(String propagation)
      Set the propagation, such as DEFAULT, REQUIRED, REQUIRES_NEW, NESTED.
      Parameters:
      propagation - name of the propagation.
      Returns:
      this transaction template instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the supplied value is invalid.

    • isolation

      TransactionTemplate isolation(int isolation)
      Set the isolation level, such as DEFAULT, REPEATABLE_READ, READ_COMMITTED, READ_UNCOMMITTED and SERIALIZABLE.
      Parameters:
      isolation - name of the isolation level.
      Returns:
      this transaction template instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the supplied value is invalid.

    • readOnly

      TransactionTemplate readOnly(boolean readOnly)
      Set whether to optimize as read-only transaction. Default is "false".

      The read-only flag applies to any transaction context, whether backed by an actual resource transaction propagation REQUIRED and REQUIRES_NEW or operating non-transactionally at the resource level SUPPORTS. In the latter case, the flag will only apply to managed resources within the application, such as a Hibernate Session.

      This just serves as a hint for the actual transaction subsystem; it will not necessarily cause failure of write access attempts. A transaction manager which cannot interpret the read-only hint will not throw an exception when asked for a read-only transaction.

      Returns:
      this transaction template instance.

    • timeout

      TransactionTemplate timeout(int timeoutSeconds)
      Set the transaction timeout to apply, as number of seconds.

      Exclusively designed for use with propagation REQUIRED and REQUIRES_NEW since it only applies to newly started transactions.

      Note that a transaction manager that does not support timeouts will throw an IllegalArgumentException.

      Parameters:
      timeoutSeconds - transaction timeout in seconds.
      Returns:
      this transaction template instance.

    • newContext

      TransactionContext newContext(boolean suspendMode) throws jakarta.persistence.PersistenceException
      Creates a new transaction context based on the current configuration of this transaction template.
      Parameters:
      suspendMode - whether the transaction is created to be used in suspend mode.
      Throws:
      jakarta.persistence.PersistenceException - if the transaction subsystem raised an issue, such as an invalid configuration.

    • currentContext

      default Optional<TransactionContext> currentContext()
      Returns the current transaction context if any.
      Returns:
      the current transaction context if any.

    • contextHolder

      ThreadLocal<TransactionContext> contextHolder()
      Returns the thread local that holds the current transaction context.
      Returns:
      thread local that holds the current transaction context.

    • execute

      <R> R execute(@Nonnull TransactionCallback<R> action, @Nonnull TransactionContext context) throws jakarta.persistence.PersistenceException
      Executes the specified action in the scope of a transaction. Exceptions raised by the action will be relayed and will mark the transaction as rollback only.
      Type Parameters:
      R - result object type.
      Parameters:
      action - action to preform in the scope of a transaction.
      Returns:
      result object.
      Throws:
      jakarta.persistence.PersistenceException - if the transaction subsystem raised an issue.
